g.text: Learn the core JDBC interfaces for database operations

g.data-narration: A Statement Object is a JDBC interface that sends SQL commands to databases and retrieves results for data manipulation.
g.text: A Statement object is a JDBC interface that represents a SQL statement to be executed against
a database. It sends SQL commands and retrieves results for data manipulation.

g.data-narration: Statement Objects execute SQL queries, perform CRUD operations, and retrieve data through ResultSet objects from databases.
g.text: • Execute SQL queries (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
• Perform CRUD operations on database records

g.data-narration: JDBC provides three Statement types: Statement for simple queries, PreparedStatement for parameterized queries, and CallableStatement for stored procedures.
The basic Statement interface executes simple SQL queries without parameters. Use createStatement() method to create it from a Connection.
PreparedStatement pre-compiles SQL with placeholders, preventing SQL injection and improving performance through query caching.
g.text: String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?";

g.data-narration: CallableStatement executes stored procedures in the database, supporting IN/OUT parameters for complex business logic operations.
Key Statement methods include executeQuery() for SELECT, executeUpdate() for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, and close() to free resources.
This complete code example demonstrates establishing a database connection, creating a Statement, executing queries, and properly closing resources.
Always close Statement and Connection objects in finally blocks, use PreparedStatement for security, and consider batch processing for bulk operations.
Statement Objects are essential JDBC interfaces: Statement for simple queries, PreparedStatement for security, and CallableStatement for stored procedures.
g.text: • Statement Objects execute SQL commands against databases
• PreparedStatement prevents SQL injection and improves performance
• Always close resources and handle SQLExceptions properly
